evals.name: commit-message-format
evals.prompt: I just added a new handoff skill to the ai-agent-skills repo. The skill saves session state to JSON. What should my commit message look like?
evals.expected_output: Produces a commit message following Conventional Commits format: feat(handoff): <imperative subject>. Subject is lowercase, no trailing period, under 72 chars. May include an optional body explaining why. Does NOT include a Co-Authored-By trailer.
expectations: Commit message uses the 'feat' type for a new skill
Subject is in imperative mood and lowercase

evals.name: branch-naming
evals.prompt: I'm about to start work on GitHub issue #17 which is about fixing a bug where the vault-scribe skill generates invalid frontmatter when the title contains special characters. What should I name my branch?
evals.expected_output: Produces a branch name matching the pattern fix/<issue-number>-<slug>: fix/17-vault-scribe-frontmatter. Uses fix/ prefix because it is a bug. Slug is 2-4 words, lowercase, hyphenated, no stop words.
expectations: Branch name uses the 'fix/' prefix (bug label maps to fix/)
Branch name includes the issue number 17
Slug is 2-4 hyphenated lowercase words derived from the issue description
No version dots used — hyphens only
Branch name does not exceed reasonable length

evals.name: changelog-entry
evals.prompt: I just merged a PR that added a new readme-writer skill. Issue was #9. The URL is https://github.com/psenger/ai-agent-skills/issues/9. Write the correct CHANGELOG entry for the Unreleased section.
evals.expected_output: Produces a bullet under '### Added' subsection inside '## [Unreleased]'. Format: '- **readme-writer** — <what was added and why>. ([#9](https://github.com/psenger/ai-agent-skills/issues/9))'
expectations: Entry goes under '### Added' (new skill = Added subsection)
Entry uses an em dash separator after the skill name
Issue link is at the end in format ([#9](url))
Description is plain English, one bullet only
